purpose: Customer support ticket management. Tracks all inbound support requests, engineering escalations, and follow-up tasks.
csm_usage: CSMs view tickets for their accounts, create follow-up tasks, and escalate tickets to engineering or tier-2.

fields.name: requester_id
fields.description: Zendesk user ID of the submitter

fields.name: organization_id
fields.description: Zendesk organization ID. NOT linked to Salesforce Account ID.

fields.name: tags
fields.description: Issue category tags (e.g., billing, api-error, data-sync, performance)

csm_owner.description: Free-text field for CSM name. NOT a lookup to Salesforce user. Manually entered and often blank.

salesforce_account_id.description: Manually entered Salesforce Account ID. Present on ~60% of tickets. Not enforced or validated.

contract_sla_tier.description: Manually entered by support agent. Often missing or incorrect because agents cannot access DocuSign.

fields.name: due_at
fields.description: SLA due date — calculated from ticket priority, NOT from the account's actual contract SLA tier.

Ticket.integration_note: Zendesk calculates SLA based on ticket priority field, which does not reflect the actual contract SLA tier in DocuSign. An Enterprise account with a 2-hour SLA may get a 4-hour SLA response if the agent entered 'high' instead of 'urgent' priority.

fields.name: notes
fields.description: Free-text notes about the organization. NOT synced from Salesforce.

fields.name: external_id
fields.description: Can hold Salesforce Account ID, but field is optional and infrequently populated.

known_gaps.description: Zendesk Organization is not linked to Salesforce Account. Two separate identity systems with no programmatic sync. Mapping is manual and incomplete.
known_gaps.impact: CSM must switch between tools to understand full account context. Tickets can exist for an account with no Salesforce linkage.

known_gaps.description: Contract SLA tier from DocuSign is not available in Zendesk. SLA clock is based on ticket priority entered by the support agent, which may not match contract terms.
known_gaps.impact: SLA breach risk on Enterprise accounts. Over-servicing of Basic accounts.

known_gaps.description: CSM ownership of accounts is not enforced in Zendesk. The csm_owner custom field is manually populated and frequently blank.
known_gaps.impact: CSMs must search by organization name to find their accounts' tickets. No automated alert to CSM when a new ticket is opened by their account.

known_gaps.description: Follow-up tickets created in Zendesk are not visible in Salesforce task list or Outlook calendar. CSMs use a personal Google Sheet to track upcoming follow-ups.
known_gaps.impact: Follow-up obligations are invisible to management and fragile to CSM turnover.

sample_tickets.subject: API rate limit errors — integration down
Fleet data not syncing — 6 hour lag observed
User cannot export reports — permission error
